recommendations: Implement a 30-minute SSH session timeout to prevent prolonged brute-force attacks and reduce the risk of successful authentication attempts. | Conduct an immediate network scan to identify any open ports or services that may be vulnerable to exploitation by the detected Brute Force Attack. | Configure the production SSH server to use IP-based access control lists (ACLs) to restrict access from the identified source IP address and implement rate limiting on login attempts. | Activate the firewall to block incoming traffic on the destination port used by the SSH server, ensuring that no further brute-force attacks can be launched against the system. | Restore the system to a known good state using a recent backup, and then perform a thorough vulnerability assessment and patch management to ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches.

Immediately block the source IP address (198.51.100.23) from accessing the affected system to prevent further potential exploitation. | Conduct a thorough analysis of the detected SQL injection payload and review logs to identify any other suspicious activity or indicators of additional attacks. | Implement an immediate patch for the vulnerable application, ensuring that all necessary security updates are applied to prevent future exploitation. | Notify the incident response team and relevant stakeholders about the incident, including the risk level and potential impact on assets (9/10 criticality). | Restore affected systems and services to a known good state, verifying that no data was compromised during the incident, and document lessons learned for future improvement.

Implement immediate email quarantine and block the source IP mail.external-domain.com to prevent further phishing attempts. | Conduct a thorough analysis of the detected incident, including reviewing logs and network traffic, to confirm the nature of the attack and gather evidence for future investigation. | Notify affected employees and provide guidance on safe email practices, such as verifying sender information before clicking links or opening attachments. | Update internal HR portal credentials securely using multi-factor authentication (MFA) to prevent unauthorized access in case of a future phishing attempt. | Conduct a post-incident review with the incident response team to assess the effectiveness of the response and identify areas for improvement, including updating security policies and employee training programs.

Conduct a thorough analysis of the incident using the evidence provided, including network logs and system files modified by the standard user account. This will help to understand the scope of the incident and identify potential entry points for future attacks (Detection & Analysis). | Immediately terminate the affected standard user account and create a new, limited account with restricted privileges. Ensure that all administrative commands are executed through a privileged account with least privilege access. This will prevent further unauthorized access to critical systems (Containment). | Review system logs and audit trails to identify any other potential security incidents or suspicious activity related to the detected incident. Also, verify that all necessary patches and updates have been applied to the affected server to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities (Detection & Analysis). | Reboot the affected critical server in a controlled environment to ensure that no further unauthorized access can occur. This will also provide an opportunity to inspect system files and logs for any other signs of compromise (Eradication). | Conduct a thorough review of user privileges, access controls, and security awareness training to identify potential vulnerabilities that may have contributed to the incident. Document lessons learned and implement changes to prevent similar incidents in the future (Recovery)

Immediately activate the incident response plan and notify relevant stakeholders, including management and security teams. | Isolate the affected file server from the network to prevent further data exfiltration and gather more evidence. | Conduct a thorough analysis of the detected incident using available tools and resources, including network traffic capture and log analysis. | Implement temporary controls to monitor and block outgoing traffic to the external IP address, and consider escalating the alert to upstream security teams for further assistance. | Restore affected systems and data from backups, if applicable, and conduct a thorough review of the incident to identify root causes and implement corrective actions to prevent similar incidents in the future.
